ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage original operation manual for the "Rampage U.R. 3 Player" arcade game,
ads.description: Vintage original operation manual for the "Rampage U.R. 3 Player" arcade game, published by Bally Midway Mfg Co. Game Title: Rampage (1986). Publisher: Bally Midway Mfg Co. Gameplay: Players control giant monsters (George, Lizzie, and Ralph) and destroy cities while battling military forces. Manual Type: This is an original operation manual with schematics for the arcade game. Significance: Rampage is a classic arcade game known for its unique monster-themed gameplay and has spawned numerous sequels and adaptations, including a 2018 film. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage Operation, Maintenance and Service Manual" for the classic arcade game, Kangaroo
ads.description: Vintage Operation, Maintenance and Service Manual" for the classic arcade game, Kangaroo. Game Overview: Kangaroo is a 1982 arcade game by Sun Electronics (distributed in North America by Atari) where the player controls a mother kangaroo, Mama Roo, to rescue her joey from mischievous monkeys. Gameplay: Mama Roo must navigate platforms and ladders, punching monkeys and collecting fruit while avoiding their attacks. Manual Purpose: The manual provides instructions for operating, maintaining, and servicing the Kangaroo arcade machine, including illustrated parts lists and schematics. Legacy: Kangaroo was later ported to various home consoles, including the Atari 2600 and 5200, and has been released digitally as part of Hamster Corporation's Arcade Archives series. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage Midway's Space Invaders Parts Catalog" from October 1978, for Game No. 739. All schematics i
ads.description: Vintage Midway's Space Invaders Parts Catalog" from October 1978, for Game No. 739. All schematics included. About Space Invaders: Space Invaders is a classic fixed shooter arcade game released in 1978 by Taito in Japan and licensed to Midway Manufacturing for distribution in North America. Gameplay: Players control a laser cannon at the bottom of the screen, moving horizontally to shoot down waves of descending aliens and earn points. Cultural Impact: The game was revolutionary for its time, influencing countless other games and shaping popular culture, leading to a shortage of 100-yen coins in Japan due to its immense popularity. Parts Catalog: The catalog shown in the image would contain details and diagrams of the various components used in the Midway Space Invaders arcade machine, assisting with repairs and maintenance. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage owner's manual for the Golden Axe arcade game.
ads.description: Vintage owner's manual for the Golden Axe arcade game. If you are interested in purchasing this or similar items, here's some information: Golden Axe arcade game manuals: are available for sale online, primarily on platforms like eBay, where you can find original installation/repair manuals. Full-size Golden Axe arcade machines: are also available for purchase, both pre-owned and new, from various sellers including those specializing in arcade machines. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage Universal Kit Installation Instructions manual for the Atari Badlands arcade game
ads.description: Vintage Universal Kit Installation Instructions manual for the Atari Badlands arcade game. This manual is for installing a kit that converts an existing arcade machine into the Atari Badlands game. The Atari Badlands game is a post-apocalyptic racing game with guns, similar to Atari's earlier Super Sprint and Championship Sprint games. The manual includes instructions for installation, self-test procedures, maintenance and troubleshooting, and illustrated parts lists. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee. [hidden information]

ads.description: As you’ve heard, we will officially be sunsetting the Golden Tee LIVE platform later this year, completing our 5-year transition from GT LIVE to the Golden Tee PGA Tour Edition. All Golden Tee LIVE games will lose online connectivity on November 1st. To help create a smooth transition, IT is offering 2027 update software protection earlier than ever this year (typically August 1st). Starting now, any PGA full kit or dedicated cabinet order will receive a FREE 2027 update this September! Note: Free update foe Commercial Units only If your cabinet is still in good condition, our PGA Full Kit option is for you! Each full kit includes a control panel with the trackball, buttons, NFC, and 5” touchscreen pre-assembled, creating a simple conversion. https://www.gatorcade.com/product/pga-clubhouse-full-kit/

ads.author: John Phillips
ads.title: Vintage Arcade Machine – Working – Original 1980 Conversion Cabinet
ads.description: Up for sale is a vintage Ms. Pac-Man arcade machine in good working condition. This is an original early-80s unit, not a modern reproduction. This machine started life as a cabinet and was professionally converted to Ms. Pac-Man using a period-correct conversion kit (common practice in arcades at the time). The game runs on an original Pac-Man board with the Ms. Pac-Man daughterboard installed. Details: Fully playable Ms. Pac-Man Original CRT monitor (picture looks good for its age) Classic joystick + buttons Cabinet is solid with normal wear from age Side art present (shows some wear, see photos) Original wiring and internal components largely intact Includes original-style operator settings card inside Condition: Works and plays Some cosmetic wear consistent with age (scratches, fading, etc.) Not fully restored – great candidate for restoration or to enjoy as-is Notes for collectors: This is a Pac-Man to Ms. Pac-Man conversion, not a factory-dedicated Ms. Pac-Man cabinet. These were widely used in arcades in the early 1980s and are part of authentic arcade history. Pickup only – located in New Hope AL. Bring help, this is heavy. Message me with any questions or if you want additional photos or video of it running.

There is also an adapter board to connect a zapper light gun, and finally it comes with 4 boards. 1-a nintendo vs board with a multi kit that plays 8 games 2-a nintendo vs board that has Mach Rider and DR Mario 3-an atari tetris board (jamma) 4-a Mario Bros boards.

ads.author: David Bolton
ads.title: Pinball machine Hokus Pokus 1975 owned since 1979
ads.description: Edit March 20 We moved into new place and something happened along the way where it is no longer resetting. When you hit the reset button it makes a noise like it's going from one to two player but nothing resets. I know it's something minor. I have lowered the price considerably. Had every switch , blade and contact filed and cleaned and machine shopped out in 2021 by Terri Irwin , a long time respected pinball mechanic in Ocala. Has a few lights out. Only issue is sometimes the score reel does not completely set over and you have to hit the button again to restart it. One of those Ghost in the machine kind of things that she just couldn't figure out . I have owned this game since 1979 when I received it as a kid. You might have seen this up for sale in the past and then the listing disappeared. This was a period of time when I needed money for something so I put it up for sale but then something else came through and I took it down because I didn't really want to sell it. At this point in life it is mostly just nostalgia. I just don't play it anymore. It is very fun game I am just bored with it after 45 years and want something different. Very open to trades for a newer pinball machine plus cash on my end. Thanks for looking! Nine zero four four four four one four three nine.. Dave

ads.author: Michael Sedovic
ads.title: Silent Scope 2 Arcade Machine – Fully Working, FRAM Upgrade (No Suicide Chip)
ads.description: Silent Scope 2: Dark Silhouette arcade cabinet in excellent working condition. Everything works exactly as it should. The screen is bright, the scope is accurate, and controls are responsive. Major upgrade, no more failure risk. This machine has been upgraded with FRAM (FM18W08-PG), eliminating the original Timekeeper RAM / “suicide chip” issue that causes many of these to fail over time. This means no battery dependency, no data loss, and long-term reliability. Features: Fully tested and 100% working Original dedicated cabinet (not a conversion) Scope tracks accurately Strong picture and sound Clean and well-maintained This is a plug-and-play arcade classic, ideal for a home game room or collector. Asking $1,400 OBO Located in Buford Pickup only – I can help load These are getting harder to find in fully working condition with this upgrade. Serious inquiries only

I don’t know anything about these but I did talk to an arcade dealer and he said that it’s likely inference or the sound board. This machine does have the ability to be paired with another machine. Will consider trading up or down for a slot machine, pinball machine, vintage gas pump, man cave stuff.
